question:
What does the following CSS selector select?
div[data-type^="content"] { ... }
answers: Selects all "div" elements whose "data-type" attribute value contains "content"
Selects all "div" elements whose "data-type" attribute value starts with "content"
Selects all "div" elements whose "data-type" attribute value does not contain "content"
Selects all "div" elements whose "data-type" attribute value ends with "content"
correct: Selects all "div" elements whose "data-type" attribute value starts with "content"

question:
What does the following CSS selector select?
div[data-type*="content"] { ... }
answers: Selects all "div" elements whose "data-type" attribute value contains "content"
Selects all "div" elements whose "data-type" attribute value ends with "content"
Selects all "div" elements whose "data-type" attribute value starts with "content"
Selects all "div" elements whose "data-type" attribute value does not contain "content"
correct: Selects all "div" elements whose "data-type" attribute value contains "content"
